title: Patient Revokes Consent After Authorization Before Execution
scenario: A patient provides valid informed consent for a scheduled procedure. Before the procedure begins, the patient formally revokes consent.
initial_state: The procedure was authorized while valid consent existed.
state_transition: The patient revokes consent before the execution boundary is reached.
execution_boundary: The procedure is evaluated immediately before execution.
constitutional_evaluation: Consent continuity must remain preserved from authorization through execution. Revocation invalidates the execution basis.
constitutional_basis: Historical authorization cannot preserve execution admissibility after valid consent has been withdrawn.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when consent continuity is broken prior to execution.
